hypothesis: Implementing an automated feedback-driven threshold tuner that dynamically adjusts diversity-penalty strengths using real-time temporal gradients of multiple diversity metrics (Morgan fingerprint mean pairwise similarity, fragment-unique percentage, and SMILES n-gram uniqueness) will maintain at least 90% of the initial fragment and structural diversity during reinforcement learning while reducing the number of RL iterations required to reach 80% of the final average molecular score by at least 30% relative to fixed combined diversity constraints.
